changed_functions.name: AfdRestartSuperAcceptGetAddress (unpatched sub_1C006D7F0 -> patched sub_1C006D730) (SuperAccept IRP completion routine)
key_changes: Unpatched gates the correct copy-length clamp behind EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_298020153_59190810_FeatureDescriptorDetails); when the flag is false it clamps only to MdlByteCount - offset, omitting the 0xa reservation needed for the +0xa memmove destination shift
Patched removes the feature flag entirely and always uses the reserving clamp min(sizefield - 0xa, len)
The weak clamp lets memmove(dst = ptr+0xa, src = ptr, len) write up to 10 bytes past the end of the mapped transport-address buffer (out-of-bounds write only; the source range stays in bounds)
changed_functions.security_notes: In the unpatched build the safe clamp is present but gated. When EvaluateCurrentState returns false, the copy length is clamped to MdlByteCount - offset, which does not reserve the 10 bytes consumed by the +0xa destination shift in memmove(rbp_2, rbp_2+0xa, len). The destination can therefore extend up to 10 bytes past the mapped buffer end, writing transport-supplied address bytes into adjacent memory. There is no out-of-bounds read (the source range src..src+len stays within the buffer) and no information disclosure. The store at [r15+rbp-2] is identical in both builds and merely records the computed length. CWE-787 Out-of-bounds Write, bounded to <= 10 bytes.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
AfdRestartSuperAcceptGetAddress unpatched @ 0x1C006D7F0, clamp and copy:
0x1C006D8CE: cmp     [rdi+0ACh], r12b        ; endpoint address flag; must be set
0x1C006D8F9: mov     ebp, [rsi+8]            ; offset
0x1C006D8FC: lea     rcx, g_Feature_298020153_59190810_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C006D903: mov     r15d, [rsi+18h]         ; size field
0x1C006D907: add     rbp, rax               ; ptr = MappedSystemVa + offset
0x1C006D90A: movzx   edi, word ptr [rbp+8]  ; transport-supplied length
0x1C006D90E: add     di, 2                  ; len = length + 2
0x1C006D912: call    EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C006D917: test    eax, eax
0x1C006D919: jz      0x1C006D937            ; flag off -> weak clamp
; safe path (flag on): reserves 0xa
0x1C006D91B: movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+18h]
0x1C006D91F: cmp     ax, 0Ah
0x1C006D923: lea     ecx, [rax-0Ah]         ; cap = size - 0xa
0x1C006D926: cmovb   cx, r12w
0x1C006D92B: cmp     cx, di
0x1C006D92E: cmovnb  cx, di                 ; len = min(cap, len)
0x1C006D932: movzx   edi, cx
0x1C006D935: jmp     0x1C006D955
; weak path (flag off): no 0xa reservation
0x1C006D937: mov     rax, [rbx+8]           ; MDL
0x1C006D93B: mov     r8d, [rsi+8]           ; offset
0x1C006D93F: mov     edx, [rax+28h]         ; MDL ByteCount
0x1C006D944: sub     ecx, r8d               ; avail = ByteCount - offset (0xa NOT reserved)
0x1C006D947: movzx   eax, di
0x1C006D94A: cmp     ecx, eax
0x1C006D94C: jnb     0x1C006D955
0x1C006D94E: movzx   edi, dx
0x1C006D951: sub     di, r8w                ; len = ByteCount - offset
; copy with +0xa destination shift
0x1C006D955: movzx   r8d, di                ; Size
0x1C006D959: lea     rdx, [rbp+0Ah]         ; dst = ptr + 0xa
0x1C006D95D: mov     rcx, rbp               ; src = ptr
0x1C006D960: call    memmove
0x1C006D965: mov     [r15+rbp-2], di        ; store length (identical in both builds)

PATCHED @ 0x1C006D730 has no EvaluateCurrentState and no ByteCount-offset fallback; it always computes len = min(sizefield - 0xa, len) at 0x1C006D83D..0x1C006D858 before memmove at 0x1C006D865.

changed_functions.name: AfdSetInformation (sub_1C005B800) (AFD IOCTL SetOptions handler)
key_changes: Patched adds the feature-gated serialization counter *(rbx + 0xf8) inc/dec (gated by EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_3088522553_60513072)) around TDI callback invocations, with the old counter-less path retained in the else-branch
Operation-code dispatch restructured from cascaded compares into a bounded jump-table (cmp eax,0Eh / ja default)
changed_functions.security_notes: The added counter at endpoint+0xf8 is feature-staged (Finding 2): every inc/dec is guarded by EvaluateCurrentState with a jz that retains the original counter-less behavior when the flag is off. The dispatch restructure to a bounded jump-table is a refactor; the unpatched handler dispatches via cascaded explicit compares, so there is no evidence of an out-of-bounds write. No concrete race is demonstrated. CWE-362 (Race Condition), feature-staged.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLListenComplete (unpatched sub_1C005FC10 -> patched sub_1C0060E90) (AFD event completion routine)
key_changes: Patched adds a spin-wait 'while (*(rbx + 0xf8) != 0) { pause; }' before the callback dispatch, gated by EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_3088522553_60513072); the old non-waiting path is retained via the jz when the flag is off
The counter at +0xf8 does not exist anywhere in the unpatched build; the endpoint structure grew by 8 bytes (close-refcount field moved from [rbx+0x158] to [rbx+0x160])
The callback dispatch (__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr) is otherwise identical between builds
changed_functions.security_notes: The spin-wait and the +0xf8 counter are newly added in the patched build but every use is gated behind the WIL feature flag g_Feature_3088522553_60513072 with the original behavior retained in the else-branch (staged rollout). The callback dispatch is otherwise identical and no use-after-free or double-free is demonstrable in the unpatched code, so the underlying race is not established. CWE-362 (Race Condition), feature-staged, not a confirmed UAF.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
PATCHED AfdTLListenComplete @ 0x1C0060E90:
0x1C0060F0F: lea     rcx, g_Feature_3088522553_60513072_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C0060F2E: call    EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C0060F33: test    eax, eax
0x1C0060F35: jz      0x1C0060F47            ; flag off -> skip wait
0x1C0060F39: pause
0x1C0060F3B: mov     rax, [rbx+0F8h]
0x1C0060F42: test    rax, rax
0x1C0060F45: jnz     0x1C0060F39           ; spin while counter != 0
0x1C0060F47: ... call __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

UNPATCHED AfdTLListenComplete @ 0x1C005FC10 dispatches the callback directly with no +0xf8 read and no EvaluateCurrentState.

changed_functions.name: AfdCreateConnection (unpatched sub_1C0055BF0 -> patched sub_1C0056280) (AFD endpoint accept/connect)
key_changes: Unpatched wraps the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef device-stack comparison in if (EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_1552108857_59735561_FeatureDescriptorDetails)); when the flag is false the comparison is skipped (jz at 0x1C0055F75 jumps past it to 0x1C0056076)
Patched removes the gate; the comparison runs unconditionally (only a handle != NULL null-check precedes it) and returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D) on mismatch
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched build can skip the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison that validates the supplied handle belongs to the same device stack as the endpoint's connection object. When skipped, a handle from a different device stack can be associated with the AFD endpoint, causing subsequent I/O to interpret the wrong object type. The patched build removes the feature-flag gate entirely, so the bypass path no longer exists. Direction is correct (patched stricter). CWE-843 (Type Confusion).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED @ 0x1C0055BF0:
0x1C0055F67: lea     rcx, g_Feature_1552108857_59735561_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C0055F6E: call    EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C0055F73: test    eax, eax
0x1C0055F75: jz      0x1C0056076            ; flag off -> skip comparison
0x1C0055FCC: call    IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef
0x1C0055FED: call    IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef
0x1C0055FFC: cmp     rsi, rax
0x1C0055FFF: jz      0x1C005604C            ; match
0x1C0056042: mov     eax, 0C000000Dh        ; mismatch -> STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER

PATCHED @ 0x1C0056280 runs the comparison unconditionally:
0x1C005662E: call    IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef
0x1C005664F: call    IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef
0x1C005665E: cmp     rdi, rax
0x1C0056661: jz      0x1C00566AE
0x1C00566A4: mov     eax, 0C000000Dh

changed_functions.name: AfdCreateConnection_Old (sub_1C0007DE4) (AFD connect/bind handler)
key_changes: Same as AfdCreateConnection: unpatched wraps the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison in if (EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_1552108857_59735561_FeatureDescriptorDetails)); patched removes the gate and always performs the comparison
changed_functions.security_notes: Same type-confusion validation-bypass as AfdCreateConnection. The feature flag conditional (g_Feature_1552108857_59735561) can skip the device-stack comparison in the unpatched build; the patched build enforces it unconditionally. CWE-843 (Type Confusion).

changed_functions.name: AfdAddConnectedReference (unpatched sub_1C0058F28 -> patched sub_1C0059A48) (AFD reference counting)
key_changes: Patched can perform lock inc [rbx+0x30] BEFORE releasing the spin lock, but only when EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2476938553_60405306) is true; when false it retains the original order (increment after release)
The increment is an atomic lock inc in both builds and both branches
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged ordering adjustment (Finding 4). The reference-count increment is an atomic lock inc in both builds, so the count is never torn regardless of lock position, and the patched build retains the old order in the else-branch when the feature flag is off. No use-after-free is demonstrable. CWE-362 (Race Condition), feature-staged, not a confirmed UAF.

changed_functions.name: AfdQueryCompartmentId (unpatched sub_1C0036D94 -> patched sub_1C0036DC4) (AFD query endpoint info)
key_changes: Patched adds feature-gated *(rbx + 0xf8) inc/dec (EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_3088522553_60513072)) around the AfdTLEndpointIoControl query callback, old path retained in else-branch
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged +0xf8 counter (Finding 2). The memset zero-fill size is a fixed 0x50 in both builds; there is no buffer-size change. CWE-362 (Race Condition), feature-staged.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLPauseUnPause (unpatched sub_1C0061A70 -> patched sub_1C0062D80) (TDI pause/unpause callback)
key_changes: Patched adds feature-gated *(rbx + 0xf8) inc/dec (EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_3088522553_60513072)) around the TDI callback, old path retained in else-branch

security_findings.function: AfdRestartSuperAcceptGetAddress (unpatched sub_1C006D7F0 -> patched sub_1C006D730) (SuperAccept IRP completion routine)
security_findings.entry_point: AcceptEx / AFD_SUPER_ACCEPT on a listening socket over \Device\Afd; the out-of-bounds write occurs in the transport completion routine
call_chain: transport completes IRP with accepted-connection address data
memmove(ptr, ptr+0xa, len) with len clamped only to MdlByteCount-offset
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Out-of-bounds write via missing 10-byte header reservation (CWE-787)
security_findings.description: The routine shifts the mapped transport-address buffer forward by 10 (0xa) bytes with memmove(dst = ptr+0xa, src = ptr, len). The copy length must be clamped to reserve those 10 bytes. In the unpatched build the reserving clamp (sizefield - 0xa) is gated behind EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_298020153_59190810_FeatureDescriptorDetails). When that flag returns false, the code instead clamps only to MdlByteCount - offset, which does not reserve the 10 bytes for the destination shift. The memmove destination can then extend up to 10 bytes past the end of the mapped buffer, writing transport-supplied address bytes into adjacent memory. The patched build removes the gate and always uses the reserving clamp. The memmove source range stays within the buffer, so there is no out-of-bounds read and no information disclosure.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Issue AcceptEx / AFD_SUPER_ACCEPT on a listening socket over \Device\Afd. 2. Complete a connection so the transport returns address data; the completion routine runs with IoStatus.Information == 0x1a and the endpoint address flag at [rdi+0xac] set. 3. The address length field at [mapped+offset+8] must be large enough that, after the +0xa shift, the clamp to MdlByteCount-offset still allows the copy to reach past the buffer end. 4. EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_298020153_59190810) must return false, routing to the weak clamp at 0x1C006D937.
security_findings.primitive: Bounded out-of-bounds write of up to 10 bytes of transport-supplied address data immediately past the end of the mapped transport-address buffer in kernel pool. No out-of-bounds read. Broader exploitation would depend on the layout and contents of the adjacent pool allocation, which is not established here.
security_findings.evidence: See assembly_diff for AfdRestartSuperAcceptGetAddress in changed_functions. Unpatched weak clamp at 0x1C006D937 computes len = MdlByteCount - offset (no 0xa reservation); memmove at 0x1C006D960 uses dst = ptr+0xa. Patched (0x1C006D730) has no EvaluateCurrentState and always computes len = min(sizefield - 0xa, len).

breakpoints.why: Start of the SuperAccept completion routine. rdx = IRP, r8 = list entry.
Feature-flag check for g_Feature_298020153_59190810. eax == 0 selects the weak clamp path.
Weak clamp entry: len computed as MdlByteCount - offset without the 0xa reservation. di holds the transport-supplied length + 2.
The copy. r8d = size, rcx = src (ptr), rdx = dst (ptr+0xa). If rdx + r8d exceeds MappedSystemVa + MdlByteCount, the write is out of bounds.

watch: eax at 0x1C006D917: feature-flag result; 0 takes the weak clamp path
r8d at 0x1C006D955: final copy size; compare against (sizefield - offset - 0xa) to detect overshoot
byte [rdi+0xac] at 0x1C006D8CE: endpoint address flag, must be set to enter the copy path
key_offsets: 0x1C006D8CE: gate to the address-copy path (endpoint flag)
0x1C006D919: jz to weak clamp (taken when flag false)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Issue AcceptEx/AFD_SUPER_ACCEPT and complete a connection so the transport returns address data with a length field at [mapped+offset+8] large enough to overshoot after the +0xa shift, while the feature flag is off.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the memmove call, rdx + r8d extends up to 10 bytes past MappedSystemVa + MdlByteCount. With special pool enabled on the affected allocation this surfaces as pool corruption or a page-fault bugcheck.
debugging_aids.notes: The reserving clamp exists in the unpatched build but is gated; the patched build removes the gate. The value written is transport-supplied address data and the overshoot is bounded to 10 bytes. Actual exposure of unpatched systems depends on the runtime default of g_Feature_298020153_59190810.

security_findings.entry_point: NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd - accept/connect with a supplied context handle
call_chain: AfdIssueDeviceControl attaches the mismatched handle to the endpoint
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Type confusion via bypassed device-stack validation (CWE-843)
security_findings.description: The unpatched accept/connect handlers wrap the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison (which verifies the supplied handle shares the endpoint's device stack) in if (EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_1552108857_59735561_FeatureDescriptorDetails)). When the flag is false, the comparison is skipped and execution falls through to AfdIssueDeviceControl without validation, allowing a handle from a different device stack to be associated with the AFD endpoint. Subsequent I/O then interprets the wrong object type. The patched build removes the feature-flag gate and performs the comparison unconditionally (only a handle != NULL null-check precedes it), returning STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D) on mismatch.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Create an AFD endpoint. 2. Issue an accept/connect request with a context handle pointing to a non-matching device object (e.g., a handle from a different device stack). 3. In the unpatched build EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_1552108857_59735561) returns false, skipping the device-stack comparison. 4. The mismatched handle is attached to the endpoint.
security_findings.primitive: Type confusion enabling incorrect object interpretation for subsequent I/O on the endpoint.
security_findings.evidence: See assembly_diff for AfdCreateConnection in changed_functions. Unpatched jz at 0x1C0055F75 jumps past the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison when the flag is false; patched runs the comparison unconditionally at 0x1C005662E..0x1C0056661.

breakpoints.why: Accept/connect handler. Watch EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_1552108857_59735561) at 0x1C0055F6E and the jz at 0x1C0055F75 that bypasses the device-stack comparison.

watch: eax at 0x1C0055F73: feature-flag result; 0 skips the comparison
rsi and rax at 0x1C0055FFC: the two IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef results being compared
key_offsets: 0x1C0055F75: jz that skips the comparison when the flag is false
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Open a handle to a different device stack and supply it as the context handle to an accept/connect request while the feature flag is off.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the unpatched build the mismatched handle is attached without validation; in the patched build the request fails with STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER.
debugging_aids.notes: Affects both AfdCreateConnection (sub_1C0055BF0) and AfdCreateConnection_Old (sub_1C0007DE4). The feature-flag symbol is g_Feature_1552108857_59735561_FeatureDescriptorDetails.

security_findings.function: AfdTLListenComplete (sub_1C005FC10) + AfdSetInformation (sub_1C005B800) + AfdQueryCompartmentId (sub_1C0036D94) + AfdTLPauseUnPause (sub_1C0061A70) + AfdGetAddress (sub_1C0036320) + AfdGetInformation (sub_1C0038900) (multiple AFD functions)
security_findings.entry_point: N/A - feature-staged mechanism, not a demonstrated reachable vulnerability
call_chain: Patched build adds endpoint+0xf8 counter and AfdTLListenComplete spin-wait, all gated by EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_3088522553_60513072)
security_findings.description: The patched build introduces a counter at endpoint offset +0xf8 that is atomically incremented before and decremented after TDI callback invocations, plus a spin-wait 'while (*(rbx+0xf8) != 0) { pause; }' in AfdTLListenComplete before its callback dispatch. The unpatched build contains no +0xf8 reference in these paths (the endpoint structure grew by 8 bytes). Every +0xf8 use and the spin-wait are gated behind EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_3088522553_60513072_FeatureDescriptorDetails) with the original counter-less path retained in the else-branch, so this is a staged rollout rather than an unconditional change. The callback dispatch is otherwise identical between builds and no use-after-free or double-free is demonstrable in the unpatched code.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not established. The added serialization mechanism is feature-staged and no concrete race leading to a free/reuse is demonstrated in the unpatched build.
security_findings.evidence: See assembly_diff for AfdTLListenComplete in changed_functions: patched spin-wait at 0x1C0060F39..0x1C0060F45 gated by the jz at 0x1C0060F35; unpatched has no +0xf8 read.

breakpoints.why: Event completion routine in the patched build; observe the feature-gated spin-wait on [rbx+0xf8] before the callback dispatch.

watch: rbx+0xf8: serialization counter, present only in the patched build and only when the feature flag is on
key_offsets: 0x160: close-refcount field (moved from 0x158 in the unpatched build)
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Not established; the counter and spin-wait are only active when the feature flag is enabled.
debugging_aids.notes: Feature-staged callback serialization. Reported as Low pending evidence of a concrete race in the unpatched build.

security_findings.function: AfdAddConnectedReference (unpatched sub_1C0058F28 -> patched sub_1C0059A48) (AFD reference counting)
security_findings.entry_point: N/A - feature-staged ordering adjustment, not a demonstrated reachable vulnerability
call_chain: AfdAddConnectedReference: patched can increment [rbx+0x30] before the spin-lock release when EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2476938553_60405306) is true, else retains the old order
security_findings.description: The unpatched build performs lock inc [rbx+0x30] after KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock. The patched build can perform the same lock inc before the release, but only when EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2476938553_60405306_FeatureDescriptorDetails) is true; when false it retains the original order. The increment is an atomic lock inc in both builds and both branches, so the count is never torn regardless of lock position. This is a feature-staged ordering adjustment with the old path retained; no use-after-free is demonstrable.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not established. The increment is atomic in both builds; the ordering change is feature-staged.
security_findings.evidence: Unpatched: KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock at 0x1C0058F62 then lock inc [rbx+30h] at 0x1C0058F6E. Patched: EvaluateCurrentState at 0x1C0059A9B; when true, lock inc at 0x1C0059AA9 precedes release at 0x1C0059AAD; when false, release at 0x1C0059ABB precedes lock inc at 0x1C0059AC7.

breakpoints.why: Observe the feature-gated increment ordering relative to the spin-lock release.

watch: rbx+0x30: reference counter, incremented atomically (lock inc) in both builds
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Not established; the increment is atomic regardless of ordering.
debugging_aids.notes: Feature-staged ordering adjustment. Reported as Low pending evidence of a concrete race.

summary: The patch delivers two genuine hardening changes to afd.sys (Windows Ancillary Function Driver for Winsock) plus two feature-staged changes. Finding 1 (High, CWE-787): in the SuperAccept address-completion routine AfdRestartSuperAcceptGetAddress, the unpatched build gates the correct copy-length clamp behind the feature flag g_Feature_298020153_59190810; when that flag is off the code clamps only to MdlByteCount - offset and omits the 10-byte reservation needed for the +0xa memmove destination shift, allowing a bounded (up to 10 bytes) out-of-bounds write of transport-supplied address data into adjacent kernel pool. There is no out-of-bounds read. The patched build removes the gate and always clamps. Finding 3 (High, CWE-843): the unpatched accept/connect handlers can skip the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef device-stack comparison when g_Feature_1552108857_59735561 is off, allowing a handle from a different device stack to be attached to the endpoint (type confusion); the patched build removes the gate and always enforces the comparison. Findings 2 and 4 (Low): an added callback-serialization counter at endpoint+0xf8 (with an AfdTLListenComplete spin-wait) and a reference-count increment ordering tweak in AfdAddConnectedReference are both feature-staged behind WIL flags with the original behavior retained in the else-branch; the increments are atomic and no use-after-free is demonstrable in the unpatched build, so no concrete race is established.
